qtype: calculations with mass, quantity, money, unit conversion and rounding
tex:
Three people pick strawberries.
\newline

The strawberries are sold in boxes.
\newline

(a) (i)  On Wednesday, they pick a total of $89\,\text{kg}$ of strawberries.
\newline

        The mean mass of each strawberry is $22\,\text{g}$.
\newline

        Work out the number of strawberries they picked.
\newline

        Give your answer correct to the nearest 10.
\newline

        

         On Thursday, they pick a total of 4650 strawberries.
\newline

        They fill each box with 35 strawberries.
\newline

        How many boxes do they fill?

        

(b) On Saturday, they sell 208 boxes of strawberries at \$3.25 for each box.
\newline

    Work out how much money they receive.
\newline

    

(c) On Monday, they receive \$390 for their boxes of strawberries.
\newline

    They share this money in the ratio Alison : Bob : Jenny $= 7 : 3 : 2$.
\newline

    Work out how much money they each receive.
\newline

    

    

(d) In 2016, they picked a total of $3500\,\text{kg}$ of strawberries.
\newline

    In 2017, they picked a total of $3080\,\text{kg}$ of strawberries.
\newline

    Work out the percentage decrease in the mass of strawberries they picked from 2016 to 2017.
\newline

    

(e) An open box in the shape of a cuboid is $7\,\text{cm}$ long, $5\,\text{cm}$ wide and $4\,\text{cm}$ high.
\newline

    Complete the net of the box.
\newline

    The base of the box has been drawn for you.
\newline

stem.content:
Three people pick strawberries.
\newline

The strawberries are sold in boxes.
\newline

_rawBody:
Three people pick strawberries.

The strawberries are sold in boxes.

\begin{parts}
    \item
    \begin{subparts}
        \subpart On Wednesday, they pick a total of $89\,\text{kg}$ of strawberries.

        The mean mass of each strawberry is $22\,\text{g}$.

        Work out the number of strawberries they picked.

        Give your answer correct to the nearest 10.
        \AnswerLine[][]
        \Marks{3}

        \subpart On Thursday, they pick a total of 4650 strawberries.

        They fill each box with 35 strawberries.

        How many boxes do they fill?
        \AnswerLine[][]
        \Marks{2}
    \end{subparts}

    \item On Saturday, they sell 208 boxes of strawberries at \$3.25 for each box.

    Work out how much money they receive.
    \AnswerLine[\$][]
    \Marks{1}

    \item On Monday, they receive \$390 for their boxes of strawberries.

    They share this money in the ratio Alison : Bob : Jenny $= 7 : 3 : 2$.

    Work out how much money they each receive.

    \AnswerLine[Alison \$][]
    \AnswerLine[Bob \$][]
    \AnswerLine[Jenny \$][]
    \Marks{3}

    \item In 2016, they picked a total of $3500\,\text{kg}$ of strawberries.

    In 2017, they picked a total of $3080\,\text{kg}$ of strawberries.

    Work out the percentage decrease in the mass of strawberries they picked from 2016 to 2017.
    \AnswerLine[][\%]
    \Marks{3}

    \item An open box in the shape of a cuboid is $7\,\text{cm}$ long, $5\,\text{cm}$ wide and $4\,\text{cm}$ high.

    Complete the net of the box.

    The base of the box has been drawn for you.
    \Marks{2}
\end{parts}

tex:
(a) The bar chart shows the number of different films shown at a cinema in each of four months.
\newline

    (i)  In May, 6 films were shown and in June, 4 films were shown.
\newline

        Complete the bar chart.
\newline

         How many more films were shown in March than were shown in January?

        

(b) The cinema is open from 13$\,$30 to 23$\,$15 every day.
\newline

    Work out how long the cinema is open in one week.
\newline

    Give your answer in hours and minutes.
\newline

    

(c) One Monday afternoon, the cinema sells 24 adult tickets, 16 child tickets and 8 senior tickets.
\newline

    Work out the total amount of money received for these tickets.
\newline

    \begin{tabular}{|l|c|}
    \hline
    \multicolumn{2}{|c|}{Ticket prices} \\
    \hline
    Adult & \$10.50 \\
    Child & \$6.25 \\
    Senior & \$9.00 \\
    \hline
    \end{tabular}

    

(d) One Tuesday, there are 24 children out of the 80 people in the cinema.
\newline

    Find the percentage of these people that are children.
\newline

    

(e) (i)  A film lasts for 85 minutes.
\newline

        It starts at 19$\,$40.
\newline

        Find the time when the film ends.
\newline

        

         Another film lasts for 2 hours 45 minutes.
\newline

        It finishes 10 minutes before the closing time of 23$\,$15.
\newline

        Find the time that it starts.
\newline

        

(f) In the cinema café, Harry buys a cup of tea for \$1.85 and a cake for \$1.70.
\newline

    Work out the change he receives from a \$5 note.
\newline

    

(g) In the café, the fridge has a temperature of $4\,°\text{C}$ and the freezer has a temperature of $-17\,°\text{C}$.
\newline

    (i)  Work out the difference in these temperatures.
\newline

        

         The temperature in the freezer rises by $3\,°\text{C}$.
\newline

        Work out the new temperature in the freezer.
\newline

\newline

\newline

The cinema is open from 13$\,$30 to 23$\,$15 every day.
\newline

    Work out how long the cinema is open in one week.
\newline

    Give your answer in hours and minutes.
\newline

One Monday afternoon, the cinema sells 24 adult tickets, 16 child tickets and 8 senior tickets.
\newline

    Work out the total amount of money received for these tickets.
\newline

One Tuesday, there are 24 children out of the 80 people in the cinema.
\newline

    Find the percentage of these people that are children.
\newline

A film lasts for 85 minutes.
\newline

        It starts at 19$\,$40.
\newline

        Find the time when the film ends.
\newline

Another film lasts for 2 hours 45 minutes.
\newline

        It finishes 10 minutes before the closing time of 23$\,$15.
\newline

        Find the time that it starts.
\newline

In the cinema café, Harry buys a cup of tea for \$1.85 and a cake for \$1.70.
\newline

    Work out the change he receives from a \$5 note.
\newline

In the café, the fridge has a temperature of $4\,°\text{C}$ and the freezer has a temperature of $-17\,°\text{C}$.
\newline

Work out the difference in these temperatures.
\newline

The temperature in the freezer rises by $3\,°\text{C}$.
\newline

        Work out the new temperature in the freezer.
\newline

qtype: simplifying algebraic expressions, indices, solving linear equations, factorisation, rearranging formula
tex:
(a) Simplify.
\newline

    (i)  $16c - 5d - 4c + 4d$

        

         $4x^3 \times 2x^7$

        

(b) Solve.
\newline
    \[3x - 2 = 5x + 1\]

    

(c) Factorise completely.
\newline
    \[3x^2y - 5xy\]

    

(d) Make $r$ the subject of the formula.
\newline
    \[T = 3(r + 5)\]

content.content:
Simplify.
\newline
$4x^3 \times 2x^7$

Solve.
\newline
    \[3x - 2 = 5x + 1\]

Factorise completely.
\newline
    \[3x^2y - 5xy\]

Make $r$ the subject of the formula.
\newline
    \[T = 3(r + 5)\]

tex:
Triangles $A$, $B$ and $C$ are shown on the grid.
\newline

(a) Describe fully the **single** transformation that maps triangle $A$ onto triangle $B$.
\newline

    

(b) Describe fully the **single** transformation that maps triangle $A$ onto triangle $C$.
\newline

    

(c) Translate triangle $A$ by the vector $\begin{pmatrix} 2 \\ 5 \end{pmatrix}$.
\newline

(d) Reflect triangle $A$ in the line $x = 3$.
\newline

tex:
(a) 70 people each attempt a driving test.
\newline

    Each person repeats the test until they pass.
\newline

    The results are shown in the table.
\newline

    \begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
    \hline
    Number of attempts & Number of people \\
    \hline
    1 & 19 \\
    \hline
    2 & 17 \\
    \hline
    3 & 8 \\
    \hline
    4 & 12 \\
    \hline
    5 & 9 \\
    \hline
    6 & 5 \\
    \hline
    \end{tabular}
    (i)  Write down the mode.
\newline

        

         Calculate the mean.
\newline

        

         Jules says that the median is 3.5.
\newline

        Show that he is wrong.
\newline

        

(b) The number of attempts at a driving test and the number of driving lessons for each of 17 people are shown in the scatter diagram.
\newline

    (i)  What type of correlation is shown in the scatter diagram?

        

         One of these people is picked at random.
\newline

        Work out the probability that this person had 5 or more attempts.
\newline

        

         Draw a line of best fit on the scatter diagram.
\newline

         Another person had 15 lessons.
\newline

        Estimate their number of attempts.
\newline

content.content:
70 people each attempt a driving test.
\newline

    Each person repeats the test until they pass.
\newline

    The results are shown in the table.
\newline

\begin{center}
    \begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
    \hline
    Number of attempts & Number of people \\
    \hline
    1 & 19 \\
    \hline
    2 & 17 \\
    \hline
    3 & 8 \\
    \hline
    4 & 12 \\
    \hline
    5 & 9 \\
    \hline
    6 & 5 \\
    \hline
    \end{tabular}
    \end{center}

Write down the mode.
\newline

Calculate the mean.
\newline

Jules says that the median is 3.5.
\newline

        Show that he is wrong.
\newline
What type of correlation is shown in the scatter diagram?

One of these people is picked at random.
\newline

        Work out the probability that this person had 5 or more attempts.
\newline

Draw a line of best fit on the scatter diagram.
\newline

Another person had 15 lessons.
\newline

        Estimate their number of attempts.
\newline

qtype: number representation, ordering fractions decimals percentages, identification of number types (prime, multiple, irrational)
tex:
(a) Write in figures the number eight million and twenty three thousand.
\newline

    

(b) Write these in order of size, starting with the smallest.
\newline
    \[\frac{3}{7} \qquad 42\% \qquad 0.45 \qquad \frac{7}{17}\]

    

(c) \[\sqrt{64} \qquad 2.9 \qquad \frac{25}{13} \qquad \sqrt{97} \qquad 39 \qquad 47 \qquad 4.6^3 \qquad 111 \qquad 1.5 \times 10^6\]

    Write down a number from this list that is

    (i)  prime,

        

         a multiple of 13,

        

         irrational.
\newline

        

(d) The number, $n$, is given as 5300, correct to 2 significant figures.
\newline

    Complete this statement about the value of $n$.
\newline

    

(e) **Without using a calculator**, work out $1\dfrac{3}{4} \times 1\dfrac{2}{7}$.
\newline

    Show all your working and give your answer as a mixed number in its simplest form.
\newline

content.content:
Write in figures the number eight million and twenty three thousand.
\newline

Write these in order of size, starting with the smallest.
\newline
    \[\frac{3}{7} \qquad 42\% \qquad 0.45 \qquad \frac{7}{17}\]

\[\sqrt{64} \qquad 2.9 \qquad \frac{25}{13} \qquad \sqrt{97} \qquad 39 \qquad 47 \qquad 4.6^3 \qquad 111 \qquad 1.5 \times 10^6\]

    Write down a number from this list that is
prime,
a multiple of 13,

irrational.
\newline

The number, $n$, is given as 5300, correct to 2 significant figures.
\newline

    Complete this statement about the value of $n$.
\newline

\textbf{Without using a calculator}, work out $1\dfrac{3}{4} \times 1\dfrac{2}{7}$.
\newline

    Show all your working and give your answer as a mixed number in its simplest form.
\newline

tex:

    \hline
    $x$ & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 & 5 & 6 & 7 & 8 \\
    \hline
    $y$ & 0 & & 12 & 15 & & 15 & 12 & & \\
    \hline
    \end{tabular}

(b) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = 8x - x^2$ for $0 \leqslant x \leqslant 8$.
\newline

    

(c) Write down the equation of the line of symmetry of this graph.
\newline

    

(d) Use the graph to solve $8x - x^2 = 10$.
\newline
